issue.message: `TODO` call found; points to code which must be fixed prior to release
Missing space between "configuration" on the previous line and "for" here? Resulting string is "configurationfor".

`register` is defined both as a member in class `org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskContainer` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

`register` is defined both as a member in class `org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskContainer` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

`compileJavaTaskProvider` is defined both as a member in class `org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.plugin.mpp.KotlinJvmAndroidCompilation` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl.kotlin`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

`compileJavaTaskProvider` is defined both as a member in class `org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.plugin.mpp.KotlinWithJavaCompilation` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl.kotlin`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

`register` is defined both as a member in class `org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskContainer` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).

`register` is defined both as a member in class `org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskContainer` and an extension in package `fluxo.conf.impl`. The defined behavior for this is to use the member, but since the extension is explicitly imported into this file, there's a chance that this was not expected. (One common way this happens is for members to be added to a class after code was already written to use an extension).
A newer version of Gradle than 9.3.1 is available: 9.5.0
A newer version of com.android.library than 9.1.1 is available: 9.2.0
A newer version of com.android.lint than 9.1.1 is available: 9.2.0
A newer version of com.android.tools.build:gradle than 9.1.1 is available: 9.2.0
